title: Investment Banking Graduate Analyst - Singapore
description:
About Jefferies




Jefferies is a leading global, full-service investment banking and capital markets firm that provides advisory, sales and trading, research and wealth and asset management services. With more than 47 offices in 21 countries around the world, we offer insights and expertise to investors, companies and governments.




Our talented professionals provide a full range of advisory and underwriting services through creative and idea-driven M&A, leveraged finance, equity & equity-linked financing and restructuring & recapitalization solutions. With approximately 1,300 investment banking professionals we provide global coverage across all products and sectors.




What you can expect:




Jefferies Investment Banking Analysts have the unique opportunity to gain insight into a global, client-focused investment bank and experience an entrepreneurial environment where employees have the opportunity to make an immediate impact and are rewarded for performance. Our Analysts are exposed to Jefferies’ full range of top-tier investment banking products and leading industry expertise by working closely with senior bankers on pitches and live deals. Our lean deal team structure will not only provide you with unparalleled hands-on experience, it will help you develop lasting personal and professional relationships with peers and senior bankers that will serve as the foundation for a successful career.




All Investment Banking Analysts at Jefferies receive an enriching orientation comprising a structured training curriculum in August, once our entire 2026 Graduate Analyst cohort has joined us. Following an enriching orientation and training program, Analysts will begin working on their day-to-day responsibilities, with support from colleagues serving as both instructors and mentors. Jefferies’ collegial program also offers a series of social and educational events to further enhance your professional development and grow your network.




In addition to performing fundamental analysis and research into companies and markets, Analysts are challenged to think creatively, offer their input, and add value to the team from day one. As with everyone at Jefferies, Analysts are expected to pursue their projects with excellence, a collaborative spirit, and with the highest levels of integrity.




Analyst responsibilities may include, but are not limited to:

Performing financial valuation, discounted cash flow and multiples-based analyses
Building and using financial models
Preparing pitchbooks and live deal materials, such as offering memorandums and prospectuses
Conducting industry and product research
Analyzing detailed corporate and financial information
Participating in the execution of financings and M&A transactions




What we look for in a candidate

We look for candidates who are eager to make an impact by doing real, hands-on work and participating on active deal teams. Successful Jefferies Analysts are able to multitask and manage a variety of projects simultaneously. Candidates must demonstrate professionalism and client management skills together with strong technical, written and verbal communication skills. The work is fast paced, challenging and intellectually stimulating. The ability to work under pressure and tight deadlines, be adaptable and work both independently and as part of a team are essential traits of a successful candidate.




Although a background in accounting or finance can be helpful, we hire Analysts who have studied a variety of disciplines. A few more application particulars are enclosed below:




Candidates applying to this position must possess the right to work in Singapore (i.e.: is a Singaporean Permanent Resident or a Singaporean citizen).
Candidates who are fluent in a Southeast Asian language (e.g.: Bahasa Melayu, Bahasa Indonesia, Tagalog, Thai, Vietnamese, etc.) are encouraged to apply.
Candidates who have graduated / are graduating between October 2025 - March 2026 are eligible to apply.




Investment Banking

The Jefferies Singapore Investment Banking team focuses on the origination and execution of equity and M&A transactions for our Southeast Asian clients. Our business in Southeast Asia and Asia has a proven track record with tremendous deal momentum, which we continue to leverage to play an active role in M&A, initial public offerings and equity fundraises, cross-border financing across different sectors in the region. Our Investment Banking team works closely with teams in New York, London and other regional offices as one global team in cross-border transactions. Leveraging our unparalleled global resources and network, Jefferies’ Singapore platform is well positioned to capitalize on the continued growth of Southeast Asia and Asia economies.




Community & Engagement, Reasonable Accommodations and COVID




At Jefferies, we have made a commitment to build a culture that provides opportunities for all employees regardless of our differences, and supporting a workforce that is reflective of the communities where we work and live. As a result, we are able to pool our collective insights and intelligence to provide fresh and innovative thinking for our clients.




Jefferies is an equal employment opportunity workplace. We practice equal opportunity for all, regardless of race, religion, ancestry, color, gender, pregnancy, age, physical or mental disability or medical condition, national origin, marital, family and social status, sexual orientation, gender identity or expression, genetic information, religion, trade union membership or any other factor protected by law. We are committed to hiring the most qualified applicants and complying with all federal, state, and local equal employment opportunity laws. In accordance with applicable law, we are dedicated to finding reasonable accommodations for candidates and employees’ religious practices and beliefs, as well as any mental health or physical disability needs. Please inform your recruiter should you require accommodations throughout your application process.

title: TH Business Development (Warehouse) - Fresh Graduate
description:
About The Team

Why Join SCommerce BD?

In this role, you aren’t just an employee—you are a business consultant for our sellers. We have many different teams and we want to meet you first to see which area best fits your unique talents!

Job Description

What You Will Get to Do:

As a BD Associate, you will be the "bridge" between Shopee and our partners. Your potential scope includes;:


Grow Big Brands: Manage a portfolio of sellers (including famous Supermarket brands). You will advise them on strategy, negotiate deals, and help them increase their sales.
Hunt for New Partners: Identify and reach out to exciting new sellers. You will pitch Shopee’s services (like our fulfillment and retail models) and bring them onto our platform.

Fresh Graduates: You have a degree in any field and are hungry to start your career in a fast-paced tech company.

Analytical Thinkers: You like working with data (Excel) to understand "the why" behind the numbers.
Fast Learners: Things change quickly here! You need to be able to adapt and learn new systems in a heartbeat.
The "Can-Do" Spirit: You are proactive, organized, and love hitting targets.


Why You’ll Love Working Here


Fast-Track Growth: You will learn more in one year at Shopee than in three years elsewhere.
Impact: See your hard work result in real sales and growth on the Shopee app.
Mentorship: Be guided by experienced leaders in the Thai e-commerce industry.

We are seeking driven and talented individuals to join our Management Trainee Programme. This 1-year program is designed to cultivate leadership potential by providing intensive training, hands-on experience, and in-depth knowledge in a specific department. title: Junior Engineer-Process Engineering
description:
Junior Engineer-Process Engineering Gradiant India Private Limited Chennai, India 4/1/26 Process Engineering

About The Organization

Gradiant is a Different Kind of Water Company. With a full suite of differentiated and proprietary end-to-end solutions for advanced water and wastewater treatment powered by the top minds in water, the company serves its clients’ mission-critical operations in the world’s essential industries, including semiconductors, pharmaceuticals, food & beverage, lithium and critical minerals, and renewable energy. Gradiant’s innovative solutions reduce water used and wastewater discharged, reclaim valuable resources, and renew wastewater into freshwater. The Boston-headquartered company was founded at MIT and has over 1,400 employees worldwide.

Discover us at gradiant.com.

Job Overview

The Jr. Process Engineer plays a key role in the design, development, and optimization of water and wastewater treatment plants. This position involves contributing to proposal preparation and executing process engineering activities aligned with project and client requirements. The role requires close collaboration with sales, business development, process engineering, and new product introduction teams to ensure timely and cost-effective project delivery. The Process Engineer ensures that all work complies with company standards and fosters a culture of continuous improvement.

Responsibilities


Develop and implement innovative water and wastewater treatment processes to enhance operational efficiency and effectiveness.
Design process trains for water and wastewater treatment plants in coordination with the process engineering team.
Perform equipment design and sizing to meet process requirements.
Prepare and review detailed Process Flow Diagrams (PFDs), Heat and Mass Balances, and Piping and Instrumentation Diagrams (P&IDs).
Coordinate with cross-functional teams including business development, engineering, project management, operations, and safety during proposal preparation.
Participate in vendor technical evaluations, risk assessments, and contribute to selection decisions.
Develop comprehensive project cost estimates throughout the project lifecycle and collaborate with business development for pricing.
Deliver client proposals punctually while maintaining high standards of technical quality.
Participate in and present during customer meetings, including bid clarifications and safety/risk workshops such as HAZOP, HAZID, and FMEA.
Prepare detailed process engineering deliverables including control logic, datasheets, commissioning and pre-commissioning procedures, supporting project and engineering teams post-award.
Assist with commissioning and site support activities as required.
Maintain clear communication with all project stakeholders, providing status updates and transparency on project progress.
Utilize membrane simulation tools, statistical analysis, and data reconciliation software for design calculations.
Continuously optimize treatment processes ensuring adherence to regulatory requirements and industry best practices.
Review and approve key project documents including design bases, design packages, and equipment specifications.
Provide technical training to internal teams and external customers on treatment processes and related technologies.
Keep abreast of emerging technologies, industry trends, and evolving regulations in water and wastewater treatment.
Evaluate existing treatment processes and recommend improvements
Develop and maintain Standard Operating Procedures (SOPs) and training documentation for treatment processes.
Ensure compliance with all safety, health, and environmental regulations related to assigned activities.
Analyze customer RFQ, tender packages, and user requirement specifications to clearly identify and meet client needs
Prepare project kickoff packages and transfer detailed documentation to the execution teams.


Requirements

Qualification

Bachelor's degree, Post Graduate Diploma, or Professional Degree in Chemical or Environmental Engineering is highly desirable.

Skills


Familiarity with a variety of water and wastewater treatment technologies.
Good understanding of membrane filtration processes and membrane system design.
Strong analytical thinking and problem-solving skills.
Excellent communication and teamwork abilities.
Detail-oriented, reliable, and well-organized.
Proficiency in English (both verbal and written).
Willingness to travel as required.
Ability to work effectively in a fast-paced, high-energy, start-up environment.
Strong interpersonal skills with the ability to convey complex technical information clearly to team members and clients.
Customer-focused with the flexibility and responsiveness to meet diverse client demands.
Proactive and driven to challenge and improve existing processes and standards.
Rigorous attention to detail, ensuring accuracy and quality in all deliverables.
Ability to work independently with minimal supervision while contributing positively to team efforts.


Experience

Minimum 3 years of experience in process and application engineering within the water and wastewater sector.

Certificate

Relevant Certifications

Gradiant is an equal opportunity employer. Qualified applicants will be considered without regard to race, color, religion, creed, gender, sexual orientation, gender identity, marital status, national origin, age, veteran status, disability, or any other protected class.

title: Junior Event Producer
description:
About Us

At Bucket Listers, we're all about unleashing life’s full potential and having a blast along the way! Our goal is to encourage people to make lasting memories, forge new connections, and embark on new adventures through our platform of curated experiences. Our vibrant community of explorers relies on us to help them find the coolest events and experiences any day of the week.

We're on a mission to make magic happen, and we're searching for enthusiastic individuals to join our incredible team. We're not just dreamers; we're seasoned explorers ourselves, and we can't wait to share our infectious positivity and limitless inspiration with the world.




The Opportunity

We’re looking for a highly organized, scrappy, solutions-oriented Junior Event Producer to support the execution of our live experiences from concept to launch. This role works directly under the Senior Project Manager and plays a critical hands-on role in getting events off the ground; managing logistics, coordinating vendors, tracking timelines, and being boots-on-the-ground during installs and openings.

This is not a sideline position; you will be in the details, in the field, and often in the action, especially during launch weeks.




While Bucket Listers is a remote-first company, this position will require the candidate to maintain residency in the eastern time zone.




In this role, you will:

Support end-to-end event production across multiple cities and activations
Assist with vendor coordination (fabricators, AV, F&B partners, staffing agencies, etc.)
Track production timelines, budgets, and deliverables to ensure deadlines are met
Help manage permitting, COI collection, and venue compliance requirements
Coordinate shipments, inventory, and on-site logistics
Build and maintain run-of-show documents and operational playbooks
Support hiring, onboarding, and training of event staff
Travel to event sites for install, launch, and strike
Troubleshoot real-time issues during openings (guest flow, staffing gaps, tech hiccups, vendor delays, etc.)
Be a proactive problem-solver when things inevitably shift




Qualifications:

1–3 years of experience in event production, experiential marketing, hospitality, operations, or relative field.
Experience supporting live event launches or large-scale productions preferred
Extremely resourceful and autonomous in execution
Strong collaborator with excellent relationship-building skills
Strong relationship builder through high levels of customer service, integrity and professionalism.
Extremely organized multi-tasker who can balance multiple projects and priorities at once
Self-starter and highly motivated; capable of working independently and collaboratively within a fast-paced environment
Strong proficiency in Google Suite and project management tools
Willingness to travel 25-50% of the time
Ability to lift up to 40 lbs and work extended hours during installs
Passionate about the Bucket Listers mission




We’re in the business of fun and we are excited to collaborate with people that will add value to our mission and culture.




Bucket Listers is an equal opportunity employer and we are committed to building a diverse and inclusive team.

title: Junior Corporate Recruiter
description:
Bisnow Overview

Bisnow is the leading B2B media platform powering the commercial real estate industry. We connect CRE professionals through award-winning news, high-impact events, marketing solutions, recruiting services, and sales enablement tools that drive real business results.

With 1.7M+ subscribers and 400+ events annually across 47 markets in the U.S., Canada, the UK, Ireland, and the Netherlands, we don’t just cover the industry, we help move it forward.

We inform. We connect. We create opportunities that turn into deals. Global mindset. Hyper-local execution.

We’re looking for a driven Junior Recruiter to join our growing team and own the full-cycle recruitment process, partnering closely with executive leaders at Bisnow. In this role, you’ll proactively source top talent, build and manage new business relationships, and connect candidates with career-advancing opportunities. This is a unique opportunity for an ambitious professional to launch and grow a career within the Commercial Real Estate industry while being part of a thriving, entrepreneurial, and highly collaborative virtual business. This is a hybrid role based out of our NYC office at 123 William St. You’ll be in-office 3-4 days per week.

Key Responsibilities


Source and identify qualified candidates using a variety of recruiting tools and platforms
Screen applicants, conduct interviews, and complete reference checks
Present and communicate offers to selected candidates
Build and maintain a strong pipeline of talent aligned with key industry skill sets
Develop close relationships with candidates, guiding them toward their next career move while partnering with internal leadership to understand client needs
Proactively generate new business opportunities and leads
Engage with leading companies and executives across the Commercial Real Estate industry
Support the growth of Bisnow’s culture, community, and Diversity & Inclusion initiatives as part of the People Ops team, contributing to special projects and reinforcing company values
Partner with Hiring Managers to provide guidance and onboarding support, ensuring alignment with recruiting processes and systems



WHAT ARE WE LOOKING FOR?


Bachelor’s degree required (Communications, Psychology, Business preferred)
0 - 2 years of direct recruiting experience
Team-player, strong work ethic, no task is too small mentality
Excellent written and verbal communication skills
Attention to detail and strong organizational skills
Quick learner, who enjoys acquiring knowledge in new subject areas
Creative thinker who is analytically capable and intellectually curious
Appetite, appreciation, and a genuine interest in the Commercial Real Estate Industry
Prior Real Estate experience is not required, but a bonus



WHAT'S IN IT FOR YOU?


Competitive compensation structure!
Medical, Dental and Vision Insurance - effective the 1st of the month following your start date
Short and Long Term Disability Insurance - this includes maternity and paternity leaves as well.
401K
Flexible Spending Account
Health Savings Account
Dependent Care Account
Unlimited Vacation Days
7 days paid sick leave
9 paid Holidays
Referral Bonus Program
Pet Friendly Offices
You’ll get to work with incredibly smart, passionate, driven, ambitious, kind, caring and mindful people and will rarely experience an overload of policies, bureaucracy or toxicity (the latter, we do not tolerate)

Before applying, please read our values below. Our values describe and give insight into our culture. If you do not share the same values, this role will not be a mutually beneficial fit. If you do share these values and want to apply, we encourage you to do so.

Our values demand that we be curious, self-aware, fearless, consistent. We say yes to seemingly insurmountable challenges because we dare ourselves to push further. And we go the distance because we are individually and collectively entrepreneurial, always asking: Why can’t it be done? We find a way because we care more than the competition. We embrace team and disown ego. We are ruthlessly disciplined and unabashedly kind. We unreservedly challenge the status quo and vigorously fight for new levels of excellence. We believe the difference between good and great is exceptional communication. We obsess over creating value for our team and our customers. We push ourselves and our industry to be more inclusive, to champion diversity and to fight racism, gender bias and all forms of inequality. We never peak. We never quit. We never have excuses. We own our failures and we commit to being smarter and stronger because of them. We give our best today and even better tomorrow. We are here to win.

Bisnow will not offer sponsorship for employment status (including, but not limited to, H1-B visa status and other employment-based nonimmigrant visas) for this position. C’est rejoindre une entreprise à mission, un acteur culturel et social, un collectif de plus de 750 collaborateurs engagés dans plus de 100 métiers, unis par une même ambition : repousser les limites, innover et faire rayonner le Club, sur le terrain comme en coulisses.




Club pluriel et engagé, le PSG regroupe plusieurs disciplines de haut niveau : football, handball, judo, et s’appuie sur un centre de formation intégré, conçu pour transmettre ses valeurs et former la nouvelle génération.

Porté par l’excellence, l’innovation et l’esprit d’équipe, le Paris Saint-Germain est le club de la Nouvelle Génération, où chaque idée compte et où chaque talent peut laisser sa trace.




Votre terrain de jeu

Rattaché(e) au Branding & Production Manager F/H du Paris Saint-Germain, le Brand Presentation Officer Junior F/H aura pour principal objectif d'accompagner l'équipe dans le pilotage des différents projets éphémères et pérennes du service Brand et Production.

Il accompagne les chefs de projets dans le suivi des éléments de branding sur l’ensemble des sites, la réalisation des produits de lancement saisonniers, les appels d’offres des différents projets, les suivis de travaux de design sur nos différents sites, le suivi pour la réalisation des supports partenaires.




Description:

Vos missions :

Appels d’offres :

Recherche de nouveaux prestataires selon les besoins des projets en gestion et les sites ;
Etablissement des cahiers des charges pour la sélection des fournisseurs ;
Lancement des appels d'offres




Design et Production :

Réponse aux besoins dans l’agencement, la décoration des différents espaces des sites du club ;
Brief auprès de nos architectes et designers et/ou des agences ;
Suivi de fabrication et de pose ;
Optimiser les demandes internes et globaliser la production ;




Branding :

Accompagner les entités du club dans la bonne mise en place des différentes déclinaisons en branding liées à la marque ;
Élaboration de PPT de présentation auprès des services ;




Budgets :

Maîtrise des budgets prévisionnels (fonctionnement, investissement) et les suivre ;




Le profil que nous cherchons :

Nous cherchons un(e) professionnel(le) polyvalente et agile orienté(e) terrain animé(e) par la créativité et la rigueur.

Un profil autonome avec une bonne capacité d'anticipation, capable de passer d'un projet à l'autre, dans un écosystème très exigent.

Issu(e) d’une formation BAC+3 ou BAC+5 (université, école d'ingénieur, école de marketing ou école de commerce) vous disposez d'une expérience de 2 ans sur un poste similaire
Vous avez une parfaite maîtrise des outils pack office et bonne connaissance de Adobe
Vous avez développé une forte appétence pour la décoration d’intérieur
Vous faites preuve de flexibilité et de créativité
Vous aimez travailler sous pression et dans l’urgence

Salarié

title: Junior Valuation Analyst
description:
Bracebridge Capital, LLC is a leading alternative asset manager with approximately $12 billion of net assets under management. The firm pursues investment strategies primarily within the global fixed income markets with the objectives of capital preservation and absolute return without significant correlation to equity, interest rate and foreign exchange markets. Established in 1994, Bracebridge manages private investment funds that serve endowments, foundations, pension funds and other institutional and high-net-worth investors.

Approximately 160 employees operate from our office located in Boston’s historic Back Bay. The entrepreneurial and collaborative culture at Bracebridge rewards and supports motivated, dedicated, enthusiastic and intellectually curious individuals. We believe our firm’s greatest asset is the people who work here.

Bracebridge is currently seeking a financial markets professional with security and derivative pricing expertise in the global securitized markets, including subprime RMBS, agency and non-agency CMBS, structured bonds and other fixed and floating ABS. The Junior Valuation Analyst will work closely with the Valuation Manager as well as the firm’s traders and analysts throughout the valuation process.

Responsibilities


Evaluate the market prices used in securitized benchmark indices and other fixed income products and services on a daily, weekly and monthly basis. Analyze internally sourced pricing as well as externally sourced pricing for accuracy
Research and resolve pricing discrepancies through modeling and analysis and working with third party vendors
Work with Valuation Manager and other analysts to enhance pricing models. Conduct tolerance testing of results
Assist with the preparation of documentation for month-end reporting and regulatory audits


Requirements


Master's degree in finance, mathematics or a related field
Up to 2 years of “hands on” experience in a fixed income security valuation role
Knowledge of fixed income securities such as corporate bonds, RMBS, CMBS and ABS as well as of single name derivatives and indices
General understanding of traditional mortgage financing, yield curves and spread movements
Experience working with third party vendors such as Bloomberg, Markit, ICE, Intex and Pricing Direct
Excellent Excel skills
VBA, SQL and Python preferred
Ability to manage multiple tasks, prioritize effectively, meet deadlines and deliver high quality, error-free work in a fast-paced environment
Strong oral and written communication skills are essential
CFA, or in pursuit of a CFA, a plus


Current anticipated annual base salary range: $85,000 - $95,000

Base salary within the range will be determined by various factors including but not limited to the individual's experience, skills and qualifications.

title: Junior Front End Developer
description:
Description

Footasylum is a leading UK-based retailer specialising in streetwear and sportswear, committed to delivering innovative and trend-setting products to our customers, with a strong focus on digital transformation and data-driven decision-making.

We’re seeking a motivated Junior Front-End Developer to join our team. This entry-level role is perfect for recent graduates, career changers, or self-taught developers passionate about creating high-quality, user-friendly web experiences. You’ll collaborate closely with engineers, designers, and product teams to build responsive and accessible web applications.

The Team

The UX and Delivery team is a tight-knit, agile group responsible for a wide range of digital touchpoints across the business. We work closely together to support long-term digital delivery plans, CRO strategies, and daily BAU activities. While we collaborate regularly, each team member also works independently with strong communication skills and a shared commitment to design excellence.

About You

We are looking for someone who embodies the Footasylum values – Community, Collaborate and Challenge.

You will be familiar with responsive design and cross-browser compatibility

You will have a basic understanding of Git (commits, branching) and familiarity with platforms like GitHub

You will have a solid understanding of HTML, CSS, and JavaScript (ES6+)

Along with;


Exposure to a modern framework such as React (preferred), Vue, or similar
Awareness of web fundamentals like accessibility and performance
Strong willingness to learn and grow in a collaborative environment
Good problem-solving and communication skills


Desirable Skills


Experience with TypeScript
Basic knowledge of APIs (REST) and how front-ends consume data
Exposure to Node.js or full-stack concepts
Familiarity with CSS frameworks or tools (e.g. Tailwind CSS)
Understanding of testing basics (e.g. unit/UI testing)
Core: HTML5, CSS3, JavaScript (ES6+)
Framework: React
Styling: Tailwind CSS / modern CSS (Flexbox, Grid)
Tools: Git, npm/yarn, Vite or similar build tools
Design: Figma
APIs: REST
A/B testing & experimentation tools (e.g. Monetate)


Responsibilities


You will build and maintain responsive web interfaces using modern front-end technologies.
You will collaborate with designers and developers to implement new features.
You will translate UI/UX designs (e.g. from Figma) into clean, functional code.
You will learn and apply best practices in performance, accessibility, and SEO.
You will support the team in debugging, testing, and improving existing features.
You will contribute to component libraries and design systems.
You will stay updated on modern front-end tools and trends.


Why Footasylum?

We are one of the UK’s leading omni channel retailers, but as well as that, we’re a genuinely great place to work. We value you and your development. Our people across the business have taken on new opportunities, whether that’s stepping into a new role, moving into a different department, or building new skills through the support and training we offer. Our goal is to create a fun, supportive environment where your success matters just as much as ours, and where you have the tools, encouragement, and opportunities to achieve your goals.

Diversity

We recognise and value the importance of diversity to help make sure we have a diverse perspective when we are building services to customers and the wider business. This is great news for us. But for us, diversity is also about, building happy energised teams full of people who want to learn and want to be inspired by each other's different backgrounds and experiences.

Recruitment Process

We review applications on an individual basis, and if we feel you would be a good fit we’ll invite you for a call or Teams video for an informal chat about the role, and to see if we’re a good fit for you.

We value open and honest conversations and collaboration, giving you a chance to learn about what we are doing in an informal and friendly environment. We want to know about you and why you feel that this is the opportunity for you. Please note, this is not a remote role and our expectation is that you will be able to attend Head Office in a hybrid way, in Greater Manchester.

title: GUCCI Junior Product Developer
description:
Summary

About Gucci
Influential, innovative and progressive, Gucci is reinventing a wholly modern approach to fashion. Under the new vision of Artistic Director Demna, the House has redefined luxury for the 21st century, further reinforcing its position as one of the world’s most desirable fashion houses. Eclectic, contemporary, romantic — Gucci products represent the pinnacle of Italian craftsmanship and are unsurpassed for their quality and attention to detail. Gucci is part of the Kering Group, a world leader in apparel and accessories that owns a portfolio of powerful luxury and sport and lifestyle brands.

Job Description

Role Mission 

The Junior Product Developer will support the daily operations of the Creative Atelier and the Product Development team, supporting the smooth execution of both creative and technical activities. In this hybrid operational and development-focused role, you will contribute to the development of RTW collections, assist during fittings, and update all related documentation. You will also manage essential atelier logistics, including supplies, shipments, and IT support requests— to ensure an efficient and well-organized workflow. Working closely with the Senior Product Developer, internal departments, suppliers, and General Services, you will help maintain continuity across all stages of product development, from prototype creation to collection delivery.

Responsibilities


Manage the ordering and inventory of materials for the Creative Atelier ensuring continuous availability for tailoring activities.
Oversee the procurement and organization of office supplies, ensuring adequate stock and smooth operations.
Facilitate the creation of IT support tickets with Kering and Lectra for computer-related issues or the setting up of profiles for new hires.
Assist in handling requests for courier services and other messenger-related tasks.
Collaborate with General Services for any requests related to ordinary and extraordinary resources.
Support in attending fittings and taking notes to update documentation.
Assist in the preparation of documentation necessary for the transfer of information between pattern makers and the design team.
Organize and maintain files containing fitting information and collection details.
Support in preparing documents and project delivery timelines.


Key Requirements


Previous experience (1–2 years) in a similar role, ideally within RTW development for luxury or premium brands.
Ensure accuracy in expense reporting, shipment monitoring, and cataloging accessories.
Strong organizational and multitasking skills.
Comfortable using IT systems and creating support tickets for technical issues.
Proactive and willing to learn in a fast-paced environment
Strong ability to liaise with internal teams and external vendors for smooth operations.
Flexible to support activities and respond to urgent transportation or logistics needs.
Availability to travel occasionally within Italy and abroad.
Fluency in Italian and good command of English, both written and spoken.
Good knowledge of Excel and any relevant software packages.


Why work with us?

Gucci is committed to building a diverse workforce. We believe diversity in all its forms – gender, age, nationality, culture, religious beliefs and sexual orientation – enriches the workplace. It opens up opportunities for people to express their talent, both individually and collectively and it helps foster our ability to adapt to a changing world. As an Equal Opportunity Employer we welcome and consider applications from all qualified candidates, regardless of their background.

Regular

2026-05-11

Schedule

Full time

Guccio Gucci S.p.A.

description:
Job Description

As an Estimator II here at Honeywell, you will be responsible for developing accurate and competitive cost estimates for various projects. This role is essential in ensuring the success and profitability of our bids and supporting the growth of our organization.

In this role, you will impact the accuracy and competitiveness of our project bids, ensuring that they align with customer expectations and company standards. You will collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather project requirements and provide detailed cost estimates that support our business objectives.

Your role will also include overseeing, supervising and reviewing tasks performed by team members to ensure effective execution of work; managing end‑to‑end processes and projects for both internal and external clients with responsibility for timely and accurate delivery; issuing clear instructions and directions to team members on tasks to be performed; and mentoring and guiding junior colleagues to support their skill development, professional growth, and overall success

Responsibilities

Key Responsibilities


Collaborate with cross-functional teams to gather project requirements and develop cost estimates
Analyze project plans, specifications, and drawings to determine project scope and identify potential risks
Prepare detailed cost estimates, including labor, materials, equipment, and subcontractor costs
Review and validate subcontractor and vendor quotes to ensure competitiveness and accuracy
Support the estimation process for medium to large-scale projects
Assist in bid review meetings and present cost estimates to senior management


Qualifications

YOU MUST HAVE


2+ years of experience in estimating
Strong knowledge of construction methods, materials, and costs
Proficiency in using estimating software and tools
Ability to interpret project plans, specifications, and drawings


WE VALUE


Bachelor's degree in Engineering, Construction Management, or a related field
Attention to detail and accuracy
Ability to work under pressure and meet tight deadlines
Excellent analytical and problem-solving skills
Continuous learning and professional development mindset


About Us

Honeywell helps organizations solve the world's most complex challenges in automation, the future of aviation and energy transition. As a trusted partner, we provide actionable solutions and innovation through our Aerospace Technologies, Building Automation, Energy and Sustainability Solutions, and Industrial Automation business segments – powered by our Honeywell Forge software – that help make the world smarter, safer and more sustainable.

And we need people like you to make it happen.

We empower you to reach your potential with opportunities to make an impact to be proud of – for food, people and the planet.

On our Future Talent Programme Technical Track, you don't need to blend in. We’re hoping you stand out. As an innovator who tackles challenges and builds knowledge to help make food safe and available everywhere.

You’ll combine your technical know-how with the support you'd expect from a world-leading food processing and packaging company – helping you focus on delivering results, explore new technologies and develop fresh ideas that bring our purpose to life.

With passion, energy and courage, you’ll broaden your horizons and experience an international environment, using your unique point of view to improve food safety and quality for hundreds of millions of people.

For you, this is only the beginning.

Join us and make an impact to be proud of – for food, people, and the planet.

What You Will Do


Problem Solving: Identify and/or resolve root causes for incidents and defects with help from teammates or developers. Have an apt for curiosity to learn new technologies and business knowledge; and to apply them to solve business problem.

Result-oriented: Commitment to always deliver on time, on-scope and on-budget.
Adaptability: Flexible to work across time zones and open to new ways of working.

A variety of exciting challenges with ample opportunities for development and training in a truly global landscape
A culture that pioneers a spirit of innovation where our industry experts drive visible results
An equal opportunity employment experience that values diversity and inclusion
Market competitive compensation and benefits with flexible working arrangements

title: Senior Crew Planner - CoreOps (India) - Pune
description:
Job Purpose

The Senior Crew Planner manages the preparation, generation, and publication of monthly crew pairings, rosters, and training plans for Flight Crew and Cabin Crew. The role ensures compliance with regulatory requirements, company policies, and operational needs, mentors junior planners, and contributes to process improvement initiatives.

Key Result Responsibilities

Crew Planning & Scheduling


Lead the preparation and publication of legal, equitable, and cost-efficient monthly crew rosters.
Prepare and oversee monthly, seasonal, and annual crew pairings based on flight schedules and operational requirements.
Ensure incorporation of all required trainings, renewals, validations, and qualifications into schedules.
Analyze data for crew planning activities, including seasonal/ad-hoc flight schedules and training requirements.
Coordinate with departments for logistical arrangements inc. hotel accommodation, transportation, seat blocking and visa/APIS/APP requirements.
Arrange language-specific and gender-specific crew requirements to ensure appropriate staffing for scheduled flights.
Credit and Block hours adjustments for individual rosters
Ensures the timely arrangement of required standby crew as per operational needs.
Identify and resolve crew violations prior to roster publication
Ensures an equitable distribution of night flight.


Reporting & Compliance


Maintain up-to-date records of crew licenses, certificates, and qualifications.
Review, validate, and submit reports including crew KPIs, office duty rosters, and training reports.
Lead internal audit preparations and ensure timely resolution of findings.


Other Duties


Perform any other duties as assigned by the Supervisor or by Central Teams (Crew Planning, Flight Operations & Cabin Crew).


Qualifications (Academic, Training, Languages)


Bachelor’s degree in Information Systems, Management, Mathematics, or related field; diploma in aviation/transportation management with relevant experience may be accepted.
Knowledge of IATA principles and security standards is an advantage.
Expert in crew management systems and productivity tools.
Proficient in Microsoft Office
Very good written and verbal communication skills. Fluency in English.


Work Experience


With 4-6 years of experience in crew planning with mentoring responsibilities.

description:
About WPP Media

WPP is the trusted growth partner for the world’s leading brands. With exceptional talent, trusted data and intelligence, and world-class partnerships – all united by our pioneering agentic marketing platform, WPP Open – we help clients navigate change, capture opportunity, and deliver transformational growth.

For more information, visit wppmedia.com.

About WPP Media

WPP is the creative transformation company. We use the power of creativity to build better futures for our people, planet, clients and communities. For more information, visit wpp.com.

WPP Media is WPP’s global media collective. In a world where media is everywhere and in everything, we bring the best platform, people, and partners together to create limitless opportunities for growth. For more information, visit wppmedia.com

At WPP Media, We Believe In The Power Of Our Culture And Our People. It’s What Elevates Us To Deliver Exceptional Experiences For Both Our Clients And Each Other. In This Role It Will Be Critical To Embrace WPP & WPP Media’s Shared Core Values:


Be Extraordinary by Leading Collectively to Inspire transformational Creativity.
Create an Open environment by Balancing People and Client Experiences by Cultivating Trust.
Lead Optimistically by Championing Growth and Development to Mobilize the Enterprise.


Role Summary And Impact

As part of the Global Operations Centre (GOC), you will work closely with teams across the United States, Europe, and Asia to deliver high-impact solutions in a collaborative, multicultural environment. We are looking for an Executive - Search who will:


Interact effectively with regional teams (Europe, U.S., Asia) and be a strong communicator. 
Support Google Ads, GDN, Bing Ads, Amazon Search campaign launches and setup according to established processes.
Ensure ongoing campaign compliance to WPP established guidelines.
Monitor and report on campaign checks across channels/platforms.
Ensure data accuracy for campaign reporting tools and WPP campaign management software.
Produce daily campaign reports, including daily pacing, data inputting and providing commentary to regional Search teams.
Support in the work QA of junior team members as team grows.


Skills And Experience


Proficient in English 
1-2 years’ experience of planning and managing Google Ads/ Search campaigns. 
You have experience with SA360 (or other bid management platforms e.g. Marin, Kenshoo)  
You are proficient in Microsoft Excel and Google Sheets.
You possess strong analytical skills and a natural affinity for numbers.
You can analyse raw data, draw conclusions, and develop actionable recommendations as needed. 


Life At WPP Media & Benefits

Our passion for shaping the next era of media includes investing in our employees to help them do their best work, and we’re just as committed to employee growth as we are to responsible media investment. WPP Media employees can tap into the global WPP Media & WPP networks to pursue their passions, grow their networks, and learn at the cutting edge of marketing and advertising. We have a variety of employee resource groups and host frequent in-office events showcasing team wins, sharing thought leadership, and celebrating holidays and milestone events. Our benefits include competitive medical, group retirement plans, vision, and dental insurance, significant paid time off, preferential partner discounts, and employee mental health awareness days.  

WPP Media is an equal opportunity employer and considers applicants for all positions without discrimination or regard to characteristics. We are committed to fostering a culture of respect in which everyone feels they belong and has the same opportunities to progress in their careers.

We believe the best work happens when we're together, fostering creativity, collaboration, and connection. That's why we’ve adopted a hybrid approach, with teams in the office around four days a week. If you require accommodations or flexibility, please discuss this with the hiring team during the interview process.

Please read our Privacy Notice for more information on how we process the information you provide.

By creating intelligent and clean solutions for people and the planet

title: Investment Banking Analyst
description:
Investment Banking Analyst (Graduates and Experienced Hires) - Bengaluru

Please apply to Mumbai on our separate vacancy if that is closer to your location







Our Client

Our subsidiary CF Invest, is a dedicated recruitment firm focused on positions in the investment sector.




We are recruiting on behalf of our client, one of the bulge bracket bank and pre-eminent investment banks.




This tier one firm works with us to select the highest calibre talent in the global markets at entry and experienced entry level across their global offices on an ongoing basis through the first half of each year, with the view of onboarding immediately or upon graduation.




Our client is looking for two professionals to join their Investment Banking team in Bengaluru as an Analyst and use their skills and ideas to make an impact on the success of the bank. You will either be placed in their FIG, TMT or Energy Group or possibly another division as they also seek cross industry including but not limited to Industrials, Healthcare and Consumer.




The role requires talent to advise clients on various financial transactions, including mergers and acquisitions, equity/equity-linked and debt financings, joint ventures, restructurings, and financial investments.







Your Focus

As an Investment Banking Analyst, your focus is on supporting clients through complex capital markets and advisory transactions, including mergers, acquisitions, and capital raising. You will work closely with senior bankers to analyse opportunities, structure transactions, and help clients make informed strategic decisions.




The role is execution-driven and detail-intensive. You will build and maintain financial analyses, support transaction execution, and contribute to client materials used in live deals. You will also help assess risk, ensure adherence to regulatory and compliance requirements, and maintain the highest standards of accuracy, judgement, and professionalism.




This role suits someone who is analytically rigorous, commercially aware, and motivated by working at pace on high-stakes transactions. You will be expected to take ownership of your work, communicate clearly with colleagues and clients, and contribute meaningfully to deal delivery in a demanding, regulated environment.







Responsibilities

Support the execution of investment banking transactions, including M&A, capital raising, and strategic advisory mandates
Build and maintain detailed financial models and analyses to evaluate transactions and strategic options
Prepare client-facing materials, including pitch decks, information memoranda, and transaction documentation
Conduct company, industry, and market research to support deal origination and execution
Assist senior bankers in coordinating transaction workstreams and managing timelines
Support due diligence processes, including data analysis and coordination with internal and external stakeholders
Contribute to client development activities through research, analysis, and preparation of marketing materials
Assess transaction-related risks and support adherence to regulatory, compliance, and internal control requirements
Maintain high standards of accuracy, organisation, and confidentiality across all work
Work effectively with colleagues across banking, markets, legal, risk, and compliance teams




This role is about supporting the execution of complex investment banking transactions and helping clients make informed strategic and capital decisions.




You can absorb detailed financial and commercial information quickly, analyse companies, transactions, and market conditions with rigour, and form clear, well-reasoned conclusions. You approach problems with structure and discipline, and you are comfortable working under pressure as deals progress and priorities evolve.







Minimum Qualifications




Graduate – Analyst

Bachelor’s degree ideally in Finance, Economics, Accounting, Business, or a related discipline. We accept also individuals from any degree, especially those numerate based
Strong written and verbal communication skills, with the ability to produce clear client-ready materials
High attention to detail and strong organisational skills
Ability to work effectively under pressure and manage multiple priorities
Sound judgement and a strong understanding of risk, compliance, and regulatory expectations
We consider any degree or subject and although your grade indicates your ability to absorb data and apply it, your degree grade does not influence your chance of applying to us and being interviewed.







Process

This application shall be open until we reach a cap of applicants, so please apply early. We are hiring for our client across their key global markets.




Be sure to Follow Us on LinkedIn for updates on this vacancy and others: https://www.linkedin.com/company/cfcorp




Your first step is to apply with your LinkedIn profile and CV. We do not need a covering letter. Due to the volume of applications we receive, we are unfortunately unable to provide individual feedback.







Reward Structure

Analyst annual base salary of 125,000 and up to 150,000 United States Dollars depending on experience. Your salary will be paid in Indian Rupees and bonus in USD
Bonus program of on-target bonus of 50% of base salary and up to 125% for high-achievers
Relocation is provided by the company

title: Accountant
description:
The Company
PayPal has been revolutionizing commerce globally for more than 25 years. Creating innovative experiences that make moving money, selling, and shopping simple, personalized, and secure, PayPal empowers consumers and businesses in approximately 200 markets to join and thrive in the global economy.

We operate a global, two-sided network at scale that connects hundreds of millions of merchants and consumers. We help merchants and consumers connect, transact, and complete payments, whether they are online or in person. PayPal is more than a connection to third-party payment networks. We provide proprietary payment solutions accepted by merchants that enable the completion of payments on our platform on behalf of our customers.

We offer our customers the flexibility to use their accounts to purchase and receive payments for goods and services, as well as the ability to transfer and withdraw funds. We enable consumers to exchange funds more safely with merchants using a variety of funding sources, which may include a bank account, a PayPal or Venmo account balance, PayPal and Venmo branded credit products, a credit card, a debit card, certain cryptocurrencies, or other stored value products such as gift cards, and eligible credit card rewards. Our PayPal, Venmo, and Xoom products also make it safer and simpler for friends and family to transfer funds to each other. We offer merchants an end-to-end payments solution that provides authorization and settlement capabilities, as well as instant access to funds and payouts. We also help merchants connect with their customers, process exchanges and returns, and manage risk. We enable consumers to engage in cross-border shopping and merchants to extend their global reach while reducing the complexity and friction involved in enabling cross-border trade.

Our beliefs are the foundation for how we conduct business every day. We live each day guided by our core values of Inclusion, Innovation, Collaboration, and Wellness. Together, our values ensure that we work together as one global team with our customers at the center of everything we do – and they push us to ensure we take care of ourselves, each other, and our communities.

Job Summary:

This job is responsible for timely and effective reconciliation of all assigned General Ledger accounts, ensuring accurate clearing of reconciling items per company policy. You’ll ensure accurate posting of entries in SAP and customer accounts, research and resolve outstanding reconciling items, and apply advanced techniques to identify their sources. Additionally, you’ll participate in month-end closing activities, manage day-to-day accounting tasks, and identify process improvement opportunities in reporting, reconciliation, and analysis.

Job Description:

Essential Responsibilities:


Responsible for timely and effective reconciliation of all assigned General Ledger accounts
Ensure accurate clearing of reconciling items in accordance with Company Policy
Ensure accurate posting of general ledger entries in SAP and customer accounts
Involved in research, track, and timely resolve outstanding reconciling items and related issues
Apply advanced research techniques to accurately identify the source of reconciling items
Participate in month-end close activities
Identify and recommend process improvement opportunities in reporting, reconciliation, and analysis
Communicate with internal resources and/or processors to escalate and resolve differences
Drive technology adoption and changes to improve efficiency
Assist with escalated cases


Minimum Qualifications:


1+ years relevant experience and a Bachelor’s degree OR Any equivalent combination of education and experience.


Additional Responsibilities & Preferred Qualifications:

What you need to know about the role:

We are seeking a detail-oriented and motivated Staff Accountant to join our Record-to-Report team. This role plays a critical part in supporting accurate financial reporting, maintaining strong internal controls, and ensuring timely month-end close activities. The ideal candidate is someone who combines a solid accounting foundation with a strong desire to learn, improve processes, and collaborate across teams.

Your Impact:

The Staff Accountant will be responsible for preparing journal entries, performing account reconciliations, supporting close and reporting activities, and assisting with audits. This role provides strong exposure to core accounting processes and is well-suited for candidates looking to grow their career within a dynamic, fast-paced organization.

Your Day-to-Day:


Prepare and post routine journal entries in accordance with company accounting policies and US GAAP
Perform account reconciliations, investigate variances, follow up on open or unusual items and monitor the aging
Perform data entry and transactional accounting tasks with a high level of accuracy
Support period-end close activities, including close checklists and deliverables
Assist in maintaining process documentation, desk procedures, and close checklists
Assist with preparation of financial reports, schedules, and variance analysis
Participate in testing and validation activities related to system enhancements or upgrades
Help ensure compliance with internal controls, SOX requirements, and accounting policies
Respond to routine inquiries from internal business partners
Support internal and external audits by preparing schedules and responding to audit requests


What You’ll Bring:


Bachelor’s degree in Commerce, Accounting, Finance, or related discipline from an accredited university
1–3 years of relevant accounting experience, including internships, articleship, or entry-level roles
Strong understanding of basic accounting principles and financial statements
Hands-on experience supporting month-end close activities and account reconciliations
Proficiency in Microsoft Excel, including formulas, lookups, and basic data analysis
Strong attention to detail, documentation, and data accuracy
Ability to work effectively in a global, matrixed environment and across time zones


Preferred Qualifications:


CA (Chartered Accountant) – Inter or Final qualified, or CA Articleship experience preferred
Experience working with multinational companies or shared services
Exposure to ERP systems such as SAP (ECC or S/4HANA), Oracle, or similar finance systems
Familiarity with close management, reconciliation, or automation tools (e.g., BlackLine)
Basic understanding of internal controls, SOX compliance, and audit requirements
Strong written and verbal communication skills


Subsidiary:

PayPal

Travel Percent:

0

PayPal does not charge candidates any fees for courses, applications, resume reviews, interviews, background checks, or onboarding. When making an application directly, we will never ask you to share passwords, one-time passcodes (OTP), or verification codes. Any such request is a red flag and likely part of a scam. All communication regarding your application will come from official PayPal email domains. If you suspect fraudulent activity, please report it immediately. To learn more about how to identify and avoid recruitment fraud please visit https://careers.pypl.com/contact-us.

For the majority of employees, PayPal's balanced hybrid work model offers 3 days in the office for effective in-person collaboration and 2 days at your choice of either the PayPal office or your home workspace, ensuring that you equally have the benefits and conveniences of both locations.

Our Benefits:

At PayPal, we’re committed to building an equitable and inclusive global economy. And we can’t do this without our most important asset-you. That’s why we offer comprehensive, choice-based programs, to support all aspects of personal wellbeing—physical, emotional, and financial—delivering meaningful value where it matters most. We strive to create a flexible, balanced work culture with a holistic approach to benefits, including generous paid time off, healthcare coverage for you and your family, and resources to create financial security and support your mental health.

Who We Are:

Click Here to learn more about our culture and community.

Commitment to Diversity and Inclusion

PayPal provides equal employment opportunity (EEO) to all persons regardless of age, color, national origin, citizenship status, physical or mental disability, race, religion, creed, gender, sex, pregnancy, sexual orientation, gender identity and/or expression, genetic information, marital status, status with regard to public assistance, veteran status, or any other characteristic protected by federal, state, or local law. In addition, PayPal will provide reasonable accommodations for qualified individuals with disabilities. If you are unable to submit an application because of incompatible assistive technology or a disability, please contact us at paypalglobaltalentacquisition@paypal.com.

Belonging at PayPal:

Our employees are central to advancing our mission, and we strive to create an environment where everyone can do their best work with a sense of purpose and belonging. Belonging at PayPal means creating a workplace with a sense of acceptance and security where all employees feel included and valued. We are proud to have a diverse workforce reflective of the merchants, consumers, and communities that we serve, and we continue to take tangible actions to cultivate inclusivity and belonging at PayPal.

Any general requests for consideration of your skills, please Join our Talent Community.

We know the confidence gap and imposter syndrome can get in the way of meeting spectacular candidates. Please don’t hesitate to apply.

Trainee/Associate Engineer will oversee daily operation of support desk functions that includes taking calls as first level support, entering the reported problems in the ticketing system, solving user problems, proper escalation of open problems and dealing with the customers. Will also participate in preventative maintenance, troubleshooting, configuring and providing technical

support for end-users.

Great Candidates Will Look Like This

A team player who can work well within the team and collaborate cross-functionally, especially in a remote environment.

An excellent communicator who is efficient and effective with customers and colleagues via instant messages, email, video conferencing and in-person interactions.

Enjoy troubleshooting and problem-solving analytically.

Essential duties and responsibilities include the following: (Other duties may be assigned.)

Provide helpful and professional assistance to customers via phone, email and tickets.

Create or update support tickets as required for phone calls.

Document all calls, actions, and any relevant information in ticket notes.

Handle tickets created by customers via email.

Follow up on existing tickets proactively and within specified time frames.

Resolve or escalate tickets in a timely manner, within SLA guidelines.

Interact with other departments and teams, collaborating on tickets or solutions.

Evaluate customers’ needs and determine the appropriate action.

Troubleshoot complex technical issues with customers via phone.

Understand and follow security and other guidelines for customers’ accounts.

Process voicemails, creating or updating support tickets as necessary.

Write and send customized email responses to customers for specific situations.

Understand and adhere to Scale Computing policies and procedures.

Place outbound calls to resolve internally-detected issues.

Be professional and courteous at all times, especially when speaking or conversing with customers.

Technical Requirements

Knowledge of at least some of the following:

General computer systems

PCs, laptops, mobile devices.

Current and recent Operating Systems

Windows, Mac OS, Linux

Routing Protocols and concepts

Static and Dynamic routing, Distance, Priority, routing table, Default Gateway

Switching Protocols and concepts

VLAN, Trunks and link bonding, Spanning Tree and RSTP, MAC Address table,

ARP.

Networking Protocols and concepts

TCP, UDP, DHCP, DNS, VPN, ICMP, TELNET, SSH FTP, HTTP(s), IPSEC, SSL,etc.

Network troubleshooting tools such as PING, TRACEROUTE, NSLOOKUP, IPCONFIG, etc.


OSI Model – Advanced understanding of layers 1-4
Subnetting and Supernetting
Firewall rules and policies, Access Lists


Deep understanding of technical concepts within networking environments as well as security, risk and compliance.

Entry Level knowledge of Switches, Routers, Firewalls, and Networking in general is required. Must be detail-oriented and must be able to work well in a team environment.

Reporting Structure

Reports to Tier 1 Manager - NOC.

Education And Experience

Bachelor’s degree (B.E./B.Tech/BCA) in a technical domain such as Computer Science, Telecommunication, Electronics & Communication, Information Technology, or a related field.

Minimum experience in the networking domain is preferred. (0-2 years)

Experience Working In Helpdesk Or Technical Support Is Preferred.

Industry certifications like CompTIA Network+, CCNA, CCNP, JNCIA, Wireshark, etc. are preferred.

Perks of Acumera

Group medical insurance.

Paid company holidays.

Casual dress code

Cab facilities for employees working in shifts.

Fully stocked snacks at office.

Vibrant and Inclusive Workplace Atmosphere.

Flexible work environment and an opportunity to grow as we grow.

About Global Delivery Services

Global Delivery Services refers to EY's worldwide network of service delivery centers. The GDS team plays an important role in EY’s strategy by ensuring effective support to EY’s growth agenda.

Our journey started in 2002 with approximately 200 people. Today we stand at 80,000+ professionals in ten locations around the world. We operate in Argentina, China, Hungary, India, Philippines, Poland, Sri Lanka, Mexico, Spain and the United Kingdom.

Client service is focused on providing Consulting, Assurance, Tax, Strategy & Transactions, and Knowledge support to our clients around the world. The teams enable account teams worldwide to provide seamless, high-quality, value-added support, helping deliver exceptional client service.

Enablement Services provides cost-effective, high-skilled, and innovative services to support EY’s global and local enablement teams. Markets, BMC, AWS, Finance and Accounting, Risk Management, Procurement, People Shared Services, IT Service Delivery and IT Global Infrastructure services, are among the services offered by Enablement Services.

Our innovation specialists serve the GDS Client Service and Enablement Services teams, along with Service Lines, Core Business Services and Sectors. The team brings the desired environment, technologies and skilled teams together for facilitation, rapid prototyping and innovative thinking. The competencies offered include analytics, digital, user experience, mobile technology, infrastructure, Microsoft technologies and open innovation.

The Opportunity

We are looking for Instructional Design Consultants with expertise in System Implementation/SAP projects to join the GDS PC team. This is a fantastic opportunity to be part of a leading global professional services organisation whilst being instrumental in growth of the GDS PC team.

Your Key Responsibilities


Collaborate with SAP/Systems Implementation (SI) consultants, developers, subject matter experts (SMEs), and other key stakeholders to identify target audience training needs
Utilize ADDIE, Kirkpatrick, and other recognized models to design and develop engaging SAP/SI course content and learning activities
Determine instructional end goals and create supporting material/media for SAP/SI projects (audio, video, simulations, role-plays, games etc)
Decide on the criteria used to judge learner’s performance and develop assessment instruments
Ensure high quality deliverables are produced for the project by exhaustive internal reviews and obtain excellent feedback from the client and global project counterparts
Maintaining and updating the training materials and documentation according to the changes and updates in the SAP system and business requirements
Effectively communicate and collaborate to build strong relationships with clients and other project team members
Support development of collaterals, tools, techniques, and methodologies to develop the practice
Contribute to thought leadership development for the SAP/SI learning domain


Skills And Attributes For Success


Ability to manage ambiguity and be proactive
Extremely sharp attention to detail
Strong communication and presentation skills
Cross cultural awareness and sensitivity
High energy levels, agility and adaptability
Ability to comprehend new content and topics across different domains
Open to travel for client projects that are approved as per EY and country specific travel advisory guidelines


To qualify for the role, you must have


Bachelor’s or associate degree with 2 - 4 years of relevant experience
Proven experience and knowledge of SAP systems, modules, and functions, such as SAP FI, SAP HR, SAP MM, S4/HANA etc
Knowledge of multiple ERP systems, such as Oracle, Workday etc is a plus
Excellent knowledge of storyboarding, visual design principles, and branding guidelines
Effective writing skills (grasp and command over English)
Proficiency in MS PowerPoint and MS Excel
Ability to articulate design decisions, suggestions and provide creative direction
Proficiency with one or more course authoring and learning tools: SAP EnableNow, Articulate Storyline, Adobe Captivate, WalkMe, Camtasia, Vyond, VideoScribe
Experience developing a wide range of digital learning deliverables: Web-based learning, Videos, Interactive PDFs, Webcasts, Podcasts, nano-learnings, VILT


Ideally, you’ll also


Experience with a data-driven approach to instructional design, using both qualitative and quantitative data to optimize content efficacy
Knowledge of course development software and at least one Learning Management System


What We Look For


Learning experts in the SAP/SI domain with commercial acumen, relevant experiences and high degree of enthusiasm to adapt and learn in a fast-moving environment
Knowledge and experience of working in a cross-cultural setup


What We Offer You

At EY, we’ll develop you with future-focused skills and equip you with world-class experiences. We’ll empower you in a flexible environment, and fuel you and your extraordinary talents in a diverse and inclusive culture of globally connected teams. Learn more.

Are you ready to shape your future with confidence? Apply today.

To help create an equitable and inclusive experience during the recruitment process, please inform us as soon as possible about any disability-related adjustments or accommodations you may need.

Our Commitment: As a commitment, we persistently endeavour to embody our values, fulfil our purpose, and champion inclusiveness. Our dedication is to cultivate EY into an environment where diverse perspectives are celebrated, creating a supportive atmosphere for individuals to authentically be themselves and contribute their utmost.

Professional Development: From entry-level employees to senior leaders, we believe in continuous learning. We offer opportunities to build new skills, take on leadership roles, and connect and grow through mentorship.

People and Culture: In our dynamic workplace, diversity, equity, and inclusiveness are ingrained in our culture. We're united by a commitment to create an environment where every individual's differences are valued, practices are equitable, fostering a sense of belonging. Our shared values include integrity, respect, teaming, inclusiveness, energy, enthusiasm, courage to lead, and building relationships based on doing the right thing.

Benefits: Embark on a transformative career journey with us and indulge in a suite of premium benefits, encompassing exclusive health and wellness packages, enticing rewards, and cutting-edge learning opportunities that empower you to continually grow and excel in your professional and personal development.

How to Apply: If you are passionate to join us and are aligned with our commitment to building a better working world, we invite you to apply by completing our user-friendly form with personal and professional information and by providing your consent to data privacy.

Successful candidates advance to a competency-based interview. If mutual interest persists, a job offer awaits.

title: Business Development & Growth Associate – Fintech / Supply Chain Finance
description:
Job Title: Business Development & Growth Associate – Fintech / Supply Chain Finance

Experience: 1–3 years

Location: Bangalore (Onsite)

Employment Type: Full-Time




The Opportunity

Our client, a trade technology startup backed by a 40-year-old business house with consolidated revenues of over ₹20,000 crore, is looking for a driven and curious Business Development Associate to support revenue growth, enterprise outreach, and partnership development. This is an entry-to-mid-level role that

sits at the intersection of growth, finance, technology, and supply chain




You will work alongside senior BD and leadership teams to execute outreach strategies, qualify prospects, support deal solutioning, and help build the client’s presence across target accounts and partner ecosystems.




This is ideal for someone who is curious about trade and structured finance, eager to learn the business end-to-end, and energized by the prospect of building client relationships from the ground up.




What You’ll Own

Prospecting & Outreach

Research and identify target accounts across sectors such as manufacturing, FMCG, pharma, logistics, and retail
Execute multi-channel outreach including cold emails, cold calls, LinkedIn outreach, and structured mailer campaigns
Qualify inbound and outbound leads and schedule discovery calls for senior BD team members
Maintain and update prospect data, outreach logs, and pipeline status in the CRM on an ongoing basis




Sales Support & Deal Assistance

Support the full sales cycle from initial outreach through to discovery, solutioning, and follow-up
Prepare and assist in meeting briefs, client-facing decks, and solution summaries 
Take and circulate accurate meeting minutes and action items post client interactions
Assist in preparing proposals, term sheets, and onboarding documents




Partnerships & Ecosystem Development

Support outreach and relationship-building with banks, NBFCs, fintech platforms, and logistics providers
Assist in tracking partnership conversations, follow-ups, and co-GTM opportunities




Pipeline, CRM & Reporting Discipline

Maintain a clean, up-to-date pipeline in CRM tools (Zoho, Salesforce, or equivalent)
Ensure consistent hygiene across deal stages, contact records, and communication logs
Pull pipeline and outreach reports to support team reviews and forecasting




Market Research & Competitive Intelligence

Track developments in trade finance, supply chain credit, and working capital markets
Identify and flag relevant triggers such as fundraises, leadership changes, earnings stress, or supply chain disruptions that signal prospect readiness




Thought Leadership & Events Support

Assist in representing the client at industry events, webinars, and ecosystem forums
Support content and positioning efforts including mailers, LinkedIn posts, and outreach messaging




What You Bring

1–3 years of experience in one or more of the following:
B2B sales, enterprise sales, or business development
Banking, trade finance, fintech, or supply chain finance
Inside sales, sales development, or outbound prospecting
Demonstrated ability to drive outreach and open conversations with senior stakeholders
Strong interest in finance, working capital, and structured products, prior exposure is a plus, but a genuine desire to learn is equally valued
Comfort with high-volume outreach, follow-ups, and working in a fast-paced, target-oriented environment




Core Skills

Clear and confident communication, written and verbal
Organized and detail-oriented, with strong follow-through on commitments
Ability to research, synthesize, and present information concisely
Comfortable working across multiple accounts and priorities simultaneously
A collaborative mindset with the hustle to operate independently when needed




Tools & Systems

Working knowledge of CRM tools such as Zoho, Salesforce, or equivalent is expected
Prior hands-on experience with sales intelligence and prospecting tools, Apollo.io, Lusha, RocketReach, or LinkedIn Sales Navigator, is preferred
Familiarity with email outreach sequencing tools is a plus
Basic proficiency in spreadsheets and presentation tools




Preferred Experience

Prior exposure to trade finance, supply chain finance, working capital products, or structured credit, whether in a sales, product, operations, or client servicing capacity
Experience or strong familiarity with structured finance products including invoice discounting, channel finance, supply chain credit, or sales bill discounting
Background in fintech, NBFCs, B2B SaaS, or financial services
Experience working with or prospecting into large corporates, conglomerates, or MNCs
MBA or equivalent from a reputed institution (preferred, not required)




What You’ll Get

Competitive fixed compensation with performance-linked variable
Direct exposure to complex enterprise deal-making and structured finance from day one
Mentorship from leadership with backgrounds at McKinsey, EY and Flipkart
High ownership, fast learning curve, and a seat at the table in a growing fintech
A high-trust, high-accountability culture where your work directly shapes outcomes
